skill.name: prompting
skill.description: Prompt engineering standards for AI agents, with guidance on concise structure, context management, progressive discovery, and prompt optimization.
skill.summary: Apply context engineering principles to create clearer and more efficient AI prompts.
skill.author: AaronAbuUsama

security_audit.summary: Static weak-cryptography findings are false positives caused by ordinary prose substrings, not cryptographic code. Static external-command findings are markdown-only references and do not execute commands. No prompt injection attempt, data exfiltration, network access, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Substring Matches
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography hits occur in normal documentation text such as description, AI agent design, desired outcomes, degrades, and desired output. No evidence found of MD5, DES, SHA1, RC4, or other weak cryptographic algorithm use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are plain prompt-engineering prose and contain no cryptographic API, hash function, cipher, or implementation logic. This gives very high confidence that the analyzer matched substrings rather than real security behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Mistaken for Commands
low_findings.description: The reported command findings are documentation examples. SKILL.md references reading a supplementary file, and workflows/create-prompt.md opens a markdown code block for a prompt template. No evidence found of script execution, shell invocation, or user-controlled command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The content is markdown guidance and contains no executable script file or runtime command dispatcher. The inline read reference is not invoked by code, so command execution risk is not confirmed.

content.user_title: Improve AI Prompts With Context Engineering
content.value_statement: AI prompts often become vague, verbose, or overloaded with low-value context. This skill helps users create and refine structured prompts that are concise, actionable, and easier for agents to follow.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use prompt engineering and context engineering guidance.
Defines principles for reducing context size and improving signal-to-noise ratio.
Provides a standard prompt structure with background, instructions, examples, and constraints.
Guides creation of new prompts through a step-by-step workflow.
Guides optimization of existing prompts by reducing redundancy and sharpening instructions.
Lists common anti-patterns for verbose, vague, or overloaded prompts.
limitations: Does not include executable tools, scripts, or automated prompt testing.
Does not provide domain-specific prompt libraries for every use case.
Requires users to supply the target task, audience, and expected output.
Mentions a supplementary resource path that may not exist in every environment.

use_cases.title: Create Agent Instructions
use_cases.description: Turn a workflow goal into clear agent instructions with structured context, examples, and constraints.

use_cases.title: Optimize Existing Prompts
use_cases.description: Reduce prompt length, remove duplicated information, and convert vague language into direct instructions.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Prompting
use_cases.description: Use shared prompt sections and review checklists to make team prompts more consistent.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Simple Prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a prompt for this task: [task]. Include only the essential background, direct instructions, one short example, and clear constraints.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Prompt for Clarity
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this prompt for clarity and structure: [prompt]. Identify vague wording, missing constraints, redundant context, and unclear output requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Context Use
prompt_templates.prompt: Optimize this prompt for signal-to-noise ratio: [prompt]. Keep required context, remove low-value details, and explain each major reduction.

prompt_templates.title: Design Agent Prompt Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a prompt architecture for an AI agent that handles [workflow]. Define core instructions, context loading rules, examples, constraints, and review criteria.

output_examples.input: A long support-agent prompt with repeated policies and unclear escalation rules.
output: A shorter prompt with separate sections for role, required context, response steps, escalation triggers, and output format.
A note explaining which repeated policy details were removed.

output_examples.input: A request to create a prompt for summarizing customer interview notes.
output: A structured prompt that defines audience, source material, summary categories, tone, and constraints.
A checklist for testing whether the summary captures decisions, risks, and open questions.

output_examples.input: An agent prompt that loads a full reference document before every task.
output: A revised prompt that uses references and just-in-time loading.
Guidance on when the agent should load the detailed reference.

best_practices: Keep only context that directly improves the target output.
Use stable markdown sections for background, instructions, examples, and constraints.
Test prompts against realistic tasks and remove wording that does not change results.
anti_patterns: Adding full reference material before the agent knows it is needed.
Using vague language such as might, could, or try when direct instructions are required.
Repeating the same requirement in multiple sections without adding precision.

faq.question: What does this skill help me do?
faq.answer: It helps you create and improve prompts using concise structure, clear instructions, and better context management.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands or scripts?
faq.answer: No. The skill is markdown guidance and does not include executable scripts or automation.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance is general prompt engineering advice for AI agents and supported assistant tools.

faq.question: Does it test prompt quality automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides review workflows and checklists, but users must test outputs with their target model.

faq.question: What information should I provide when using it?
faq.answer: Provide the task goal, audience, required context, expected output, examples, and constraints.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed files show documentation-only content with no confirmed malicious behavior.
